title: Paris municipal elections: Emmanuel Grégoire and the united left achieve a resounding victory
subhed: Despite a united front from the entire right-wing and centrist parties – as well as competition on the left with La France Insoumise candidate Sophia Chikirou remaining in the race – the Socialist candidate delivered the united left its most impressive victory in Paris since 2001.

title: French ex-PM Lionel Jospin dies aged 88
subhed: Jospin, a Socialist who introduced the 35-hour work week and civil partnerships for gay couples, was head of government from 1997 to 2002 before losing to the far right in presidential polls.

title: Slovenia PM claims election win as results show neck and neck finish
subhed: Robert Golob, Slovenia's incumbent liberal prime minister, claimed victory, with more than 99% of votes counted. Suspicion of foreign interference shook the campaign, with authorities probing whether an Israeli intelligence firm was behind secretly recorded videos suggesting alleged graft in Golob's government.

title: French municipal elections: Full results in every city, town and village
subhed: French voters elected more than 34,000 mayors on March 15 and 22. Use our search engine to find the winner in any municipality.

title: Live map of results
subhed: Follow the results of the second round of the municipal elections in real time with our interactive maps.

subhed: Abu Dhabi is seeking to develop new logistical routes from Sudan's neighboring countries, such as Ethiopia and the Central African Republic, to support the Rapid Support Forces.

title: French bitcoin evangelical Eric Larchevêque wants his right-wing voice to be part of public debate
subhed: Known to the public as a judge on a television show, the French bitcoin pioneer wants to win over the masses to the cryptocurrencies that made him wealthy. With the 2027 presidential election approaching, this libertarian has embraced increasingly radical ideas.

title: Polluted drinking water takes center stage in Denmark's election campaign
subhed: The presence of nitrates and pesticides in drinking water catchment sites and groundwater has become a major concern for Danes, who are also questioning the environmental impact of industrial pig farming. Bans on certain treatments are now under consideration.
